text:
One foggy evening a boy named Tommy walked home singing jaunty tunes. Across the street lay Aunt Aggie’s house. He liked it because she always had cookies. Aunt Aggie waved. He waved back. Tommy tiptoed inside, humming louder. There was popcorn. There was wine…and a rocking horse named Gnasher.

Gnasher kicked Tommy when he sat. Tommy fell. Gnasher trotted away. Into the nursery. Tommy climbed. It became his hideout. Every night tales. Once he imagined being a pirate. Another night he imagined he had wings.

They were just stories Tommy.

The next day. Aunt Aggie came looking worried. Tommy looked nervous. She smiled.

“Nothing much…I just…” But she didn’t finish.

“What?” Tommy shouted.

Aunt Aggie chuckled.

“You fell off my roof.”

Iridescent. That was all. Felt like dreams. Tommy wasn't sad. He climbed Gnasher. Never fell again. He'd
